description: Airway management actions including basic maneuvers, adjuncts, intubation, surgical airway, ventilator management, and extubation. Accessible via the dedicated Airway icon on the main encounter UI. Time-critical actions are top-level for fast access.
note: Most airway actions have no meaningful effect in appendicitis unless the patient reaches septic shock with respiratory failure. They are always available and always cost AP. Condition files define specific effects.

subcategories.name: Basic Airway Maneuvers
subcategories.description: Non-invasive positional and manual techniques to open and maintain the airway.

actions.name: Jaw Thrust
actions.description: Forward displacement of the mandible to open the airway. Preferred in suspected C-spine injury.

actions.name: Chin Lift
actions.description: Lift the chin to open the airway.

actions.name: Head Tilt / Chin Lift
actions.description: Combined head tilt and chin lift. Standard BLS airway opening maneuver.

actions.name: Repositioning
actions.description: Reposition the patient to optimize airway patency (e.g. sniffing position, lateral decubitus).

actions.name: Sniffing Position
actions.description: Align ear to sternal notch to optimize laryngoscopy view.

subcategories.name: Airway Adjuncts
subcategories.description: Devices to maintain airway patency without definitive airway.

actions.name: Nasopharyngeal Airway (NPA)
actions.description: Soft rubber airway inserted nasally. Tolerated in semi-conscious patients.

actions.name: Oropharyngeal Airway (OPA)
actions.description: Rigid oral airway. Only for unconscious patients without gag reflex.

actions.name: Laryngeal Mask Airway (LMA)
actions.description: Supraglottic airway device. Useful as rescue airway or bridge to definitive airway.

subcategories.name: Bag-Mask Ventilation (BMV)
subcategories.description: Manual ventilation with bag-valve-mask. Used before intubation or as rescue.

actions.name: Bag-Mask Ventilation (1-person)
actions.description: Single-provider BVM ventilation. EC clamp technique.

actions.name: Bag-Mask Ventilation (2-person)
actions.description: Two-provider BVM ventilation. Better seal, preferred for difficult airways.

subcategories.name: Intubation
subcategories.description: Definitive airway management via endotracheal intubation.
subcategories.note: RSI requires paralytic and sedation to be selected from medication browser before confirmation. Selecting RSI will open medication browser filtered to paralytics and sedatives.

actions.name: Rapid Sequence Intubation (RSI)
actions.description: Simultaneous administration of sedative and paralytic followed by intubation. Standard emergency intubation method.

actions.name: Video Laryngoscopy
actions.description: Intubation using video laryngoscope. Improved view, preferred for anticipated difficult airway.

actions.name: Direct Laryngoscopy
actions.description: Standard intubation using direct laryngoscope (MAC or Miller blade).

actions.name: Fiberoptic Intubation
actions.description: Awake fiberoptic intubation. Used for known or anticipated difficult airway in a conscious patient.

actions.name: Nasotracheal Intubation
actions.description: Blind nasal intubation. Used when oral intubation is not possible.

subcategories.name: Surgical Airway
subcategories.description: Last-resort surgical airway access when intubation has failed.

actions.name: Cricothyrotomy
actions.description: Emergency surgical airway via incision through the cricothyroid membrane. Used in cannot-intubate cannot-oxygenate (CICO) situations.

actions.name: Tracheostomy
actions.description: Surgical creation of a tracheostomy for long-term airway management.

actions.name: Needle Cricothyrotomy
actions.description: Emergency percutaneous needle decompression through cricothyroid membrane. Temporary measure.

subcategories.name: Ventilator Settings
subcategories.description: Mechanical ventilator management for intubated patients. Only available after intubation is established.

actions.name: Set Mode: Volume Control (VC-AC)
actions.description: Volume control assist-control ventilation. Set tidal volume, rate, PEEP, FiO2.

actions.name: Set Mode: Pressure Control (PC-AC)
actions.description: Pressure control assist-control ventilation. Set inspiratory pressure, rate, PEEP, FiO2.

actions.name: Set Mode: SIMV
actions.description: Synchronized intermittent mandatory ventilation. Allows spontaneous breaths between mandatory breaths.

actions.name: Set Mode: Pressure Support (PSV)
actions.description: Pressure support ventilation. Patient triggers all breaths, vent provides pressure boost. Used for weaning.

actions.name: Adjust Tidal Volume
actions.description: Adjust delivered tidal volume (mL). Lung protective: 6 mL/kg IBW.

actions.name: Adjust Respiratory Rate
actions.description: Adjust ventilator respiratory rate (breaths per minute).

actions.name: Adjust PEEP
actions.description: Adjust positive end-expiratory pressure (cmH2O). Improves oxygenation, prevents alveolar collapse.

actions.name: Adjust FiO2
actions.description: Adjust fraction of inspired oxygen (21-100%). Wean to lowest FiO2 maintaining SpO2 >92%.

actions.name: Adjust I:E Ratio
actions.description: Adjust inspiratory to expiratory ratio. Normal 1:2. Inverse ratio used in severe ARDS.

actions.name: Check Plateau Pressure
actions.description: Perform inspiratory hold to measure plateau pressure. Goal <30 cmH2O for lung protection.

actions.name: Lung Protective Ventilation (Preset)
actions.description: Apply ARDSnet lung protective settings: Vt 6 mL/kg IBW, Pplat <30, PEEP per FiO2 table.

actions.name: Prone Positioning
actions.description: Place intubated patient prone for 16+ hours. Improves V/Q matching in severe ARDS.

subcategories.name: Extubation
subcategories.description: Weaning from mechanical ventilation and removal of endotracheal tube.

actions.name: Spontaneous Breathing Trial (SBT)
actions.description: Trial of pressure support or T-piece breathing to assess readiness for extubation.

actions.name: Extubation
actions.description: Remove endotracheal tube. Patient must pass SBT and meet extubation criteria.
